main_ingredients: mixed grains (e.g. brown rice, quinoa, farro, barley)
choice of three toppings (e.g. edamame, cucumber, avocado, roasted vegetables, seeds)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, representing a typical restaurant or café grain bowl portion with mixed grains, salmon, and three toppings, served without sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Salmon grain bowl (3 toppings), no sauce — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Salmon grain bowl (3 toppings), no sauce' (cr_id F08010050). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central salmon and mixed grain composite data as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain and composite grain dish analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific salmon grain bowl. GI of 45 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using mixed grain bowl analogues: mixed grain dishes (brown rice, quinoa, barley blends) typically report GI in the range 40–50. The protein and fibre content (6.55 g and 2.2 g per 100 g respectively) further attenuate glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as: GI (45) × carbohydrate per serving (34.0 g from 350 g portion) / 100 = 15.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 201 mg per 100 g; estimated 704 mg per 350 g serving
hypertension.guidance: At 201 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 704 mg per standard 350 g serving), this dish approaches one-third of the 2,000 mg daily sodium target for hypertensive individuals; patients should avoid adding sauces or salty toppings and monitor cumulative sodium intake across the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low estimated GI (~45), moderate carbohydrate 9.65 g per 100 g, dietary fibre 2.2 g per 100 g, protein 6.55 g per 100 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: The mixed grain base provides a low estimated glycaemic index (~45) and the combination of dietary fibre (2.2 g/100 g) and protein (6.55 g/100 g) from salmon attenuates postprandial glucose rise, making this a suitable meal choice for type 2 diabetes management when starchy or high-GI toppings such as corn or croutons are avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Salmon provides EPA/DHA omega-3 fatty acids; low saturated fat (~0.7 g/100 g); no added sauce
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Salmon is a rich source of cardioprotective EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids, and the low saturated fat content (~0.7 g/100 g) combined with whole grain fibre (2.2 g/100 g) supports LDL cholesterol management, making this dish a heart-healthy option consistent with cardiovascular disease prevention guidelines when sodium-contributing toppings are minimised.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: EPA/DHA omega-3 from salmon; low saturated fat; whole grain antioxidants
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Salmon-derived EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids have demonstrated anti-inflammatory effects relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; selecting anti-inflammatory toppings such as avocado, walnuts, or flaxseeds maximises benefit, while nightshade toppings should be monitored for individual tolerance.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salmon Grain Bowl (3 Toppings), No Sauce is a contemporary health-casual dish popular in Singapore café and health-food settings. It consists of a base of mixed whole grains — typically a blend of brown rice, quinoa, farro, or barley — topped with a portion of grilled or baked Atlantic salmon and a customer's choice of three toppings, which may include edamame, cucumber, avocado, roasted vegetables, seeds, or pickled items. The dish is served without any sauce or dressing, which significantly reduces sodium and added sugar content. The texture is varied: chewy whole grains, flaky tender salmon, and crisp or soft toppings depending on selection. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides 94 kcal, 6.55 g protein, 3.26 g fat, and 9.65 g carbohydrate with 2.2 g dietary fibre. The salmon contributes EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids with cardioprotective and anti-inflammatory properties. The low estimated glycaemic index (~45) and moderate carbohydrate load make it suitable for type 2 diabetes management. Sodium at 201 mg per 100 g is moderate and should be monitored in hypertensive individuals. The dish is dairy-free and halal-suitable. It is classified as a minimally processed, customisable mixed dish and is well-suited as a lunch or dinner option for individuals seeking a balanced, nutrient-dense meal.
common_user_queries: Calories in salmon grain bowl no sauce
Is salmon grain bowl good for diabetes?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this exact dish (cr_id F08010050, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and potassium are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite salmon and mixed grain data and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.7 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (1.1 g/100 g), and polyunsaturated fat (1.1 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for grilled Atlantic salmon and mixed whole grain composites, as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown for this entry. These values are approximate and should be updated when HPB publishes detailed fatty acid data. 2. Potassium (225 mg/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central salmon and whole grain data; HPB does not report potassium for this entry. 3. cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database for this composite dish and detailed breakdown data are not available in a single approved source for this exact formulation. 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on mixed grain bowl composites from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish has been published. GL per serving = 45 × (9.65 × 3.5) / 100 = 45 × 33.775 / 100 ≈ 15.2, rounded to 15.3. 5. Halal status is assumed based on ingredients (salmon, whole grains, vegetables) with no sauce; individual vendor preparation should be verified. 6. Topping variability means actual nutrient values may differ from the HPB composite figure depending on customer selections.
